title: QT Imaging outlines ~$39M 2026 revenue outlook as AMA CPT code takes effect January 1, 2027
content:
Earnings Call Insights: QT Imaging Holdings (QTI) Q1 2026

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* "The first quarter of 2026 was a highly productive period for QT Imaging as we continue to execute across commercialization, clinical validation, reimbursement, regulatory expansion and software innovation." (CEO & Director Raluca Dinu)
 	* "During the quarter, we shipped 13 QT Breast Acoustic CT systems, which was in line with our commercial distribution commitments." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "Subsequent to the close of the quarter, the maturity date of our senior secured term loan with Lynrock Lake was extended by 2 years from March 31, 2027, to March 31, 2029." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "We achieved an important milestone with the approval by the American Medical Association of a new Category III CPT code for our technology, effective January 1, 2027." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "We also received FDA 510(k) clearance for enhancements to our Breast Acoustic CT scanner and platform designed to improve visualization and imaging coverage of posterior breast tissue." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "During the quarter, we launched the QT Imaging-Olea Viewer" and "we released software version 4.5.0, a next-generation image reconstruction update that improves spatial resolution and reflection imaging." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "Revenue for the first quarter of 2026 was $6.5 million" and "as of March 31, 2026, the company had cash, restricted cash and cash equivalents of $7 million." (Chief Financial Officer Jay Jennings)

OUTLOOK

 	* "We are affirming our expectation for revenue of approximately $39 million for the year" and "this guidance is based on the expected shipment of scanners under distributor minimum order quantities as well as the early monetization of cloud-based services as we bring our platform online across clinical and research customers." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Under our distribution agreements, in addition to the 60 scanners contracted by NXC Imaging for 2026, Gulf Medical is contracted for 20 scanners in 2026" and "Al Naghi Medical is contracted for 7 scanners in 2026." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "We are discussing with NXC Imaging and Canon USA further business beyond 2026." (CEO & Director Dinu)

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* "Revenue for the first quarter of 2026 was $6.5 million" and "the increase was primarily attributable to the shipment of 13 Breast Acoustic CT scanners during the quarter." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Cost of revenue for Q1 2026 was $3.9 million" and "gross margin for the 2026 quarter" was "41%." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Total operating expenses for the first quarter of 2026 were $5 million" and "net loss for the first quarter of 2026 was $3.4 million." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "EBITDA for Q1 2026 was negative $2.5 million" and "adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2026 was negative $1.5 million." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Net cash used in operating activities during the first quarter of 2026 was $3.7 million." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "We amended our credit agreement with Lynrock Lake, extending the maturity date of the $10.1 million senior secured term loan to March 31, 2029, and increasing the interest rate from 10% per annum to 12% per annum." (CFO Jennings)

Q&A

 	* Jeffrey Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc., Research Division: "What's the regulatory status in Saudi? Is it approved?" CEO Dinu: "Saudi Arabia FDA approval is still to be received" and "we do hope within the next month or so, we're going to get our SFDA approval." 
 	* Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc., Research Division: "Would you anticipate Saudi approval this calendar year?" CEO Dinu: "Saudi Arabia FDA, Jeff, it should be within a month or so." 
 	* Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc., Research Division: "I wanted to jump over to your Canon agreement" and "tell us if there's been any updates recently as far as time line" CEO Dinu: "the contract is for 2026" and "we are discussing with NXC Imaging and Canon USA further business beyond 2026."
 	* Benjamin Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: "are there any sort of edge cases, whether it's like calcification" CEO Dinu: "calcification is the one finding critical for -- that gets discovered via our reflection" and "The 4.5.0 software version directly improves resolution for us to get calcification." 
 	* Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: "Any more to share" on "longitudinal imaging/biopharma" CEO Dinu: "we are ready to submit a manuscript for a paper with clear results that show the nonresponders of various therapies" and "the volume is clearly showing the increase or decrease with much higher precision." 
 	* Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: "Are there other discussions that are ongoing" for "new distribution agreements" CEO Dinu: "we have other 4 countries in the Gulf region we're trying to cover" and "we'll work through the year to get our paperwork together for CE Mark" and "We'll probably have that up and running Q2 next year." 

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts: slightly positive, with questions centered on commercialization timing and international regulatory gating, including "What's the regulatory status in Saudi? Is it approved?" (Cohen) and cadence/expansion prompts such as "Are there other discussions that are ongoing" (Haynor).
 	* Management: positive and forward-leaning in prepared remarks ("we remain focused on disciplined execution") and confident but conditional in Q&A on timing and geopolitics ("we do hope within the next month or so" and "As soon as the war gives us a little bit of a break, we'll start shipping in UAE"). (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* Versus last quarter: the prepared-message tone stayed opportunity-focused while Q&A shifted toward near-term execution details (Saudi/UAE timing, NXC/Canon beyond 2026), compared with the prior call’s broader platform narrative and multi-item 2026 news flow framing.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* Guidance language was unchanged, with CFO Jennings repeating "revenue of approximately $39 million for the year" in both calls.
 	* The current call added financing specifics post-quarter close, including the interest-rate change, stating the loan extension also included "increasing the interest rate from 10% per annum to 12% per annum" (CFO Jennings), while the prior call discussed balance sheet strength and prior financing activities.
 	* The current call emphasized in-quarter commercialization execution ("shipped 13 QT Breast Acoustic CT systems") (CEO & Director Dinu), while the prior call emphasized full-year 2025 scale-up ("we shipped 40 scanners") and Q4 shipment volume.
 	* Analyst focus broadened from utilization ramps and future news flow in the prior call to near-term regulatory clearance timing (SFDA) and distribution continuity beyond 2026 in the current call.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* "Saudi Arabia FDA approval is still to be received" and "As soon as the war gives us a little bit of a break, we'll start shipping in UAE." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "increasing the interest rate from 10% per annum to 12% per annum" on the Lynrock Lake term loan amendment. (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Total operating expenses" increased year-over-year, with management attributing the rise primarily to "higher employee compensation and professional and outside service costs." (CFO Jennings)

FINAL TAKEAWAY

QT Imaging management highlighted Q1 shipment execution (13 systems), reiterated full-year 2026 revenue guidance of approximately $39 million tied to distributor minimum order quantities and early cloud monetization, and pointed to reimbursement and regulatory milestones, including an AMA Category III CPT code effective January 1, 2027 and FDA 510(k) clearance for posterior tissue coverage enhancements. In Q&A, management framed Saudi regulatory clearance as near-term ("within a month or so"), described geopolitical timing constraints on UAE shipping, and said it is in discussions with NXC Imaging and Canon USA regarding business beyond the 2026 contract.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/qti/earnings/transcripts]

MORE ON QT IMAGING HOLDINGS

 	* QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. (QTI) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4904304-qt-imaging-holdings-inc-qti-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. 2026 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4904207-qt-imaging-holdings-inc-2026-q1-results-earnings-call-presentation]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. (QTI) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4888126-qt-imaging-holdings-inc-qti-q4-2025-earnings-call-transcript]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings GAAP EPS of -$0.10, revenue of $8.28M; reaffirms FY26 outlook [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4568435-qt-imaging-holdings-gaap-eps-of-010-revenue-of-828m-reaffirms-fy26-outlook]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings Preliminary GAAP EPS of -$0.10, revenue of $8.3M [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4553555-qt-imaging-holdings-gaap-eps-of-0_10-revenue-of-8_3m]

title: Clover Health Investments And 2 Other Insider Picks For Growth
content:
Over the last 7 days, the United States market has risen by 2.2%, contributing to a substantial 31% climb over the past year, with earnings expected to grow by 17% annually. In such an environment, stocks with high insider ownership can be appealing as they often indicate confidence from those closest to the company’s operations and potential for growth, making them noteworthy considerations for investors seeking opportunities in this thriving market.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In The United States

Name Insider Ownership Earnings Growth Uxin (UXIN) 35.7% 74.1% Upstart Holdings (UPST) 12.8% 58.5% QT Imaging Holdings (QTI) 26.3% 110.2% Precigen (PGEN) 11.9% 68.4% Karman Holdings (KRMN) 17% 53.2% Corcept Therapeutics (CORT) 11.8% 48.7% Clene (CLNN) 10.9% 62.2% Caledonia Mining (CMCL) 14.1% 29.6% Astera Labs (ALAB) 10.8% 31.5% AppLovin (APP) 27.4% 21.4%

Click here to see the full list of 188 stocks from our Fast Growing US Companies With High Insider Ownership screener.

Here we highlight a subset of our preferred stocks from the screener.

Clover Health Investments

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Clover Health Investments Corp. offers Medicare Advantage plans in the United States and has a market cap of $1.64 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from its insurance segment, which generated $2.21 billion.

Insider Ownership: 22.1%

Clover Health Investments is experiencing strong growth, with revenue rising to US$749.19 million in Q1 2026 from US$462.33 million a year ago and achieving net income of US$27.33 million. Despite significant insider selling recently, the company trades at a good value relative to peers and industry standards, being 90.4% below its estimated fair value. title: QT Imaging Announces Uplisting to Nasdaq
content:
NOVATO, Calif., January 27, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. (OTCQB: QTIH) ("QT Imaging" or the "Company"), a medical device company dedicated to transforming breast health management through innovative, radiation-free imaging technology, today announced that it has received approval to uplist its common stock from the OTCQB Venture Market to the Nasdaq Capital Market. The Company’s shares are expected to commence trading on Nasdaq Stock Market LLC ("Nasdaq") under the ticker symbol "QTI" tomorrow Wednesday, January 28, 2026.

"We are very pleased to have received approval to reestablish our listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market," said QT Imaging Chief Executive Officer Dr. Raluca Dinu. "This milestone represents relentless work to turn the company around, rebuild its business model, its balance sheet, cap table, rebuild the trust, and refocus on what truly matters. Our team pushed through uncertainty with discipline, resilience, and belief, supported every step of the way by stockholders who stayed with us. We returned to Nasdaq after less than one year of being delisted.

"QT Imaging exists for a simple and deeply personal reason. Women deserve better options for breast imaging, earlier answers, and care that puts their safety and dignity first. Everything we have done has been in service of bringing this technology out of the lab and into the hands of clinicians and the women they serve. As a stronger company with a clear mission, we believe a Nasdaq listing increases our visibility, expands our stockholder base, and supports long term value creation. We are proud of how far we have come and energized by what lies ahead."

QT Imaging met all Nasdaq Capital Market listing requirements, including financial, corporate governance, and regulatory criteria. The uplisting aligns with the Company’s long-term strategic vision of expanding its global footprint and advancing the Company into the future of technological advances.

QT Imaging remains committed to delivering sustained value to its shareholders through innovation, operational excellence, and disciplined growth. Investors can look forward to further updates as the Company embarks on this exciting new chapter.

About QT Imaging Holdings, Inc.

QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. is a public medical device company engaged in research, development, and commercialization of innovative body imaging systems using low frequency sound waves. QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. strives to improve global health outcomes. Its strategy is predicated upon the fact that medical imaging is critical to the detection, diagnosis, and treatment of disease and that it should be safe, affordable, accessible, and centered on the patient’s experience. For more information on QT Imaging Holdings, Inc., please visit the Company’s website at www.qtimaging.com.

Story Continues

Breast Acoustic CT™ is a trademark of an affiliate of QT Imaging Holdings, Inc.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. Such statements contain words such as "will," and "expect," or the negative thereof or comparable terminology, and include (without limitation) statements regarding the uplisting to the Nasdaq Capital Market, plans for QT Imaging, new product development and introduction, and product sales growth and projected revenues. Forward-looking statements involve certain risks and uncertainties, and actual results may differ materially from those discussed in any such statement. These risks include, but are not limited to: the Company’s ability to uplist to the Nasdaq Capital Market, including meeting the initial and continuous listing requirements; research results from the use of the QTI Breast Acoustic CT scanner, clinical studies, the ability of QT Imaging to sell and deploy the QTI Breast Acoustic CT scanner, the ability to extend product offerings into new areas or products, the ability to commercialize technology, unexpected occurrences that deter the full documentation and "bring to market" plan for products, trends and fluctuations in the industry, changes in demand and purchasing volume of customers, unpredictability of suppliers, the ability to attract and retain qualified personnel and the ability to move product sales to production levels. Additional factors that could cause actual results to differ are discussed under the heading "Risk Factors" and in other sections of QT Imaging Holding’s filings with the SEC, and in its other current and periodic reports filed or furnished from time to time with the SEC. All forward-looking statements in this press release are made as of the date hereof, based on information available to QT Imaging Holdings as of the date hereof, and QT Imaging Holdings assumes no obligation to update any forward-looking statement.
